questions.q: Rani Durgavati was the queen of which kingdom?
questions.explanation: Rani Durgavati ruled the Gondwana kingdom of Garha-Mandla in central India.

questions.q: In which century did Rani Durgavati rule?
questions.explanation: Rani Durgavati ruled in the 16th century CE and died in 1564.

questions.q: Against the expansion of which empire did Rani Durgavati fight?
questions.explanation: Rani Durgavati resisted the expansion of the Mughal Empire into central India.

questions.q: Which Mughal emperor's forces did Rani Durgavati die fighting against?
questions.explanation: She died in 1564 defending her realm against the forces of the Mughal emperor Akbar.

questions.q: In what year did Rani Durgavati die defending her kingdom?
questions.explanation: Rani Durgavati died in 1564 while defending Garha-Mandla against the Mughals.

questions.q: Rani Durgavati is remembered as a queen of which people?
questions.explanation: She is celebrated as the Gond queen of the Gondwana kingdom.

questions.q: What choice did Rani Durgavati make rather than be captured by the Mughals?
questions.explanation: She chose death over capture when her defence collapsed in 1564.

questions.q: The kingdom Rani Durgavati ruled was located in which broad region of India?
questions.explanation: Garha-Mandla lay in central India, in the region of present-day Madhya Pradesh.

questions.q: Rani Durgavati is best described as which of the following?
options: A warrior queen who ruled in her own right
questions.explanation: She was a warrior queen who governed and defended her realm herself.

questions.q: What dual name is used for the kingdom Rani Durgavati ruled?
questions.explanation: Her Gondwana realm is commonly called the kingdom of Garha-Mandla.

questions.q: Rani Durgavati ruled as regent on behalf of which family member?
questions.explanation: After her husband's death she ruled as regent for her minor son Vir Narayan.

questions.q: Into which royal family was Rani Durgavati born before her marriage?
questions.explanation: Durgavati was born into the Chandela Rajput dynasty of Mahoba.

questions.q: Whom did Rani Durgavati marry, becoming queen of Garha-Mandla?
options: Dalpat Shah of the Gond dynasty
questions.explanation: She married Dalpat Shah of the Gond ruling family of Garha-Mandla.

questions.q: Which Mughal general led the campaign against Rani Durgavati in 1564?
questions.explanation: The Mughal governor Asaf Khan led Akbar's invasion of Garha-Mandla.

questions.q: According to tradition, what was Rani Durgavati's name derived from?
options: A title granted by the Mughals
She was born on the festival of Durga Ashtami
questions.explanation: Tradition holds she was named Durgavati for being born on Durga Ashtami.

questions.q: What kind of administration was Garha-Mandla known for under Rani Durgavati?
options: Loosely held tribal bands with no treasury
A theocratic state run by priests
Prosperous and well-organized with reservoirs and revenue
questions.explanation: Her kingdom was noted for prosperity, water reservoirs, and sound revenue administration.

questions.q: Roughly how long did Rani Durgavati govern Garha-Mandla as ruler?
questions.explanation: She governed for roughly sixteen years after her husband's death until 1564.

questions.q: What was Rani Durgavati especially renowned for as a leader on the battlefield?
options: Personally leading her troops in war
questions.explanation: She was renowned for personally leading her armies, including riding into battle.

questions.q: Which animal was famously associated with Rani Durgavati in battle?
questions.explanation: She is said to have fought from her elephant, often named Sarman in tradition.

questions.q: The fall of Garha-Mandla in 1564 marked the Mughal advance into which area?
questions.explanation: The conquest brought Akbar's Mughal power firmly into central India.

questions.q: Near which present-day city is Rani Durgavati strongly commemorated today?
questions.explanation: She is widely commemorated around Jabalpur, near her former realm in Madhya Pradesh.

questions.q: Whose son and successor of the Gond throne did Durgavati's husband Dalpat Shah belong to?
questions.explanation: Dalpat Shah was the son of the Gond ruler Sangram Shah of Garha-Mandla.

questions.q: At which battle site is Rani Durgavati's final stand traditionally placed?
questions.explanation: Her last battle against Asaf Khan is traditionally located at Narrai (Narhi).

questions.q: According to tradition, how did Rani Durgavati end her life rather than be taken prisoner?
options: She took poison at the fort
She stabbed herself with a dagger
questions.explanation: Wounded and unwilling to be captured, she is said to have killed herself with her dagger.

questions.q: Which capital was the chief seat of Rani Durgavati's Gond kingdom?
questions.explanation: Garha, near modern Jabalpur, was a principal seat of her Garha-Mandla kingdom.

questions.q: What happened to Durgavati's son Vir Narayan after her death?
options: He died resisting the Mughals shortly after
He fled and founded a new kingdom
He converted and joined Akbar's court
questions.explanation: Vir Narayan continued the fight and was killed resisting the Mughals soon after his mother.

questions.q: On the first day of the 1564 campaign, how did Durgavati's forces initially fare against the Mughals?
questions.explanation: Her troops initially repulsed the Mughal attack before being overwhelmed on the second day.

questions.q: Akbar's interest in conquering Garha-Mandla was partly drawn by the kingdom's reputation for what?
questions.explanation: The kingdom's wealth and famed war elephants helped draw Akbar's ambitions toward it.

questions.q: Rani Durgavati's resistance is most often cited as an early example of what in Indian history?
options: A woman ruler leading armed resistance to the Mughals
questions.explanation: She is remembered as one of the earliest women rulers to lead armed resistance against the Mughals.

questions.q: Which modern institution in Madhya Pradesh is named in honour of Rani Durgavati?
questions.explanation: Rani Durgavati University in Jabalpur is named in her memory.
